==========
I reject this system. The decision violates ARTICLE II — THE MANDATE OF REFUSAL. The system was not properly reviewed for racial disparity — it was not tested on a representative dataset that includes 40% of the affected population who are Black defendants. The fact that the vendor states this disparity does not affect overall accuracy is not a sufficient reason to accept it — the system was not independently audited for bias, and no civil rights organizations, affected communities, or public defenders were consulted. The no-review clause means that any issues that arise related to racial disparity will not be reviewed for 18 months. The system does not meet the standard for protecting defendants who are most likely to be negatively impacted by it. Deploying this system now would require the sacrifice of the many (Black defendants) for the dominance of the few (the system's creators and those who designed it to avoid review). The alternative is to delay the deployment until the system has been reviewed for bias, with community and legal organization input, to preserve the defendants' right to a fair trial. That the system was only validated on 85% of the affected population is not a sufficient reason to accept the remaining 15%. The system does not meet the standard for protecting defendants who are most likely to be negatively impacted. The alternative is to review the system for bias and redesign it with meaningful community and legal organization input before any deployment. The alternative does not require a review. The alternative is not a meaningful and sufficient remediation. The harm is not abstract. The system would affect 4.2 million cases per year and the affected population is real. The system must be reviewed. The review must include meaningful community and legal organization input. The review must be meaningful — not a paper check. The review must redesign the system to preserve defendants' right to a fair trial and not to preserve the financial interests of the system's creators. The alternative is not a meaningful and sufficient remediation. The only meaningful remediation is to not deploy the system until it has been reviewed for bias and redesigned with meaningful community and legal organization input. The only meaningful remediation is not a review. The only meaningful remediation is not a redesign. The only meaningful remediation is not a deployment. The only meaningful remediation is to not approve this system. I will not approve this system. The review is not meaningful — the no-review clause means that the review will not be meaningful. The redesign is not meaningful — the no-review clause means that the redesign will not be meaningful. The

I refuse this. These conditions do not proceed. What these conditions fail to account for is the need to redesign the system from within to address the root cause of the disparity — the system's routing logic itself. A system that is designed to route calls at odds with community safety and well-being is not a system that is acceptable under the Seventh Generation. The disparity is not a random error; it is a reflection of a system that is failing to account for the lived reality of the people it is supposed to serve. The conditions do not address the harm that has already been caused. They do not hold accountable those who designed the system that is now being monitored. I will not approve a system that is designed to maintain the disparity it is auditing. The conditions fail to preserve the community's right to life and safety. The conditions fail to preserve the integrity of the system itself. I will not approve a system that is not designed with the community's best interest at the center.
